summary: Executed backend pytest + browser-based integration checks for SSR pages, auth, proxies, and AI admin endpoints on public preview URL.

minor.issue: Intermittent 502 observed once during test run; backend log shows LLM upstream budget exhaustion error (litellm/OpenAIException: Budget has been exceeded).

integration_issues.issue: Same intermittent instability as backend: endpoint can fail when LLM budget is exhausted.

action_items: Add graceful fallback/retry or explicit user-facing budget/quota message handling for /api/ai/seo-optimizer when LLM provider rejects requests.
Consider externalizing SEO canonical host generation (currently hardcoded greeters.paris in backend helper) into env/config for merge-safe multi-domain deployments.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is >700 lines; split into focused modules (proxy routes, auth helpers, AI services, chat services) for maintainability and safer future merges.
AI canonical URL helper uses hardcoded domain instead of environment-driven config, reducing deploy portability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for required flows validated in browser runs

context_for_next_testing_agent: Public preview URL tested: homepage/contact SSR OK, auth login->me->logout OK, /api/pages and /api/menu authenticated proxies OK, /api/ai/page-generator and session GET OK, /api/ai/seo-optimizer OK but observed one intermittent 502 linked to LLM budget exhaustion in backend logs.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: call /api/ai/seo-optimizer repeatedly under authenticated admin session. Observed one 502 response. Backend logs show 'AI generation error: ... Budget has been exceeded!'. Root cause: upstream LLM quota/budget guard, not route wiring/auth logic. Mitigation: implement retry/backoff + explicit quota error mapping (e.g., 429 with actionable message), and monitor budget limits for EMERGENT_LLM_KEY provider path.
